it's incorrect way of data conversion, see the comments for controller class

this does not belong to cps api
update package to org.onap.cps.nfproxy.api
disagree! this dat is not relevant for this test I asked Hannif to remove this in an earlier commit as it against good test practices!
I dont see any comment in the controller class, did you forget to save them?

let me clarify this a bit motre. This is a UNIT test. the Controller (currently) uses GSON to convert thee DataNode object.

Adding more data the dataNode and check string in the output is really testing GSON which is not relevant in this case. even if we used our own utility to convert DataNode then any test related to additional data (conversion) should be done as unit test in the relevant conversion utility not here!

Often I hear the argument; there is no harm doing something additional like that. Well there is: it is misleading, later developers might be confused by it. It will lead to additional maintenance costs etc. Keep test clean and tidy. that's is our motto.
since there seem s a limitation with converting a DataNode with node data (for which we need to add a unit test in MapUtilsSpec) the minimum data we need for any test with be one "leaf" and "value"
teh assert is correct but yes MapUtils should be used in teh controler
apologies, I was incorrect. It should be cm-handles
